_comment: Favor & debt ledger schema (Article XXXIV.2). A favor is NOT 'relationship += 20'. A favor is a structured debt that persists and can be called in later. Wang Lin once saved the player — that is a Major favor, never expires. Years later, the player asks to study the Ji Realm. Wang Lin reasons: 'Player once risked his life for me. I owe him. Yes.' The favor is called. The debt is honored. This feels earned because it IS earned — the simulation tracked the favor for years and surfaced it at the right moment. DATA, not Java (Article XXVI).

schema.ledger_id: string — unique ledger id, typically per-NPC: 'favor_ledger_<npc_id>'
schema.owner: string — the npc_id who owns this ledger (the one who may call favors)

favors.direction: enum — owed_to_player | owed_by_player. owed_to_player = the NPC owes the player (NPC did the favor for the player). owed_by_player = the player owes the NPC (player did the favor for the NPC).
favors.reason: string — what happened. e.g. 'Player protected Wang Lin's family from bandits while Wang Lin was absent.'
favors.origin_event_memory_tag: string — the memory_ledger tag of the event that created this favor. Links the favor to the history.
favors.created_tick: int — when the favor was incurred
favors.expires: enum — never | callable_until_resolved | decays_after_N_ticks. life_saving magnitude = never. moderate = callable_until_resolved. minor = may decay.
favors.callable: bool — can the owner demand repayment? life_saving = always callable. moderate = callable. minor = callable but socially awkward.
favors.called: bool — has the owner called this favor in?
favors.called_context: string|null — what the owner asked for when calling
favors.honored: bool|null — did the debtor honor the call? null if not yet called. true if honored. false if refused.
favors.honored_tick: int|null — when it was honored (or refused)
favors.resolution_memory_tag: string|null — memory_ledger tag for the resolution event
favors.notes: string — any context. e.g. 'Wang Lin does not call this casually — only for something of equivalent weight.'

aggregates.total_owed_to_player: int — sum of outstanding favor magnitudes (minor=1, moderate=3, major=7, life_saving=15) the NPC owes the player
aggregates.total_owed_by_player: int — sum the player owes the NPC
aggregates.net_debt: signed int — total_owed_to_player - total_owed_by_player. Matches relationship_graph.debt dimension.

minor.social_weight: the NPC will do a small favor in return without hesitation
minor.expires: decays after ~30 days if not called

moderate.social_weight: the NPC feels a meaningful obligation

examples: protected what the NPC most cares about
sacrificed significant resources for the NPC
major.social_weight: the NPC remembers and will reciprocate significantly

examples: saved the NPC from certain death
saved the NPC's family from certain death
life_saving.social_weight: the NPC's graph shifts permanently; this can be called for nearly anything

calling_mechanic.description: When the player asks the NPC for something (via a Request affordance), the NPC's reasoning (motivation_state) checks the favor ledger. If the player has an outstanding callable favor of sufficient magnitude, the NPC may call it: 'You once did X for me. I have not forgotten. I will grant this.' The favor is marked called=true, honored=true. If the player later refuses a called favor (rare — the player initiated the request), the favor is marked called=true, honored=false, and the relationship graph shifts (trust-, grievance+, the NPC may become hostile).
calling_mechanic.reasoning_example: Player asks Wang Lin to teach the Ji Realm. Wang Lin's motivation_state m_call_in_favor_ji_realm evaluates: (1) player has favor 'life_saving' outstanding, callable, not called. (2) player has sufficient cultivation. (3) player has shown restraint. Decision: YES. Call the favor. Teach. Mark favor called=true, honored=true.

bidirectional_note: The ledger is per-NPC. Wang Lin's ledger tracks what Wang Lin owes and is owed. Li Muwan's ledger tracks what Li Muwan owes and is owed. They are independent — a favor with Wang Lin does not transfer to Li Muwan (unless canon says so, e.g. spouses may share certain debts — flagged per-NPC).
